title: Peppery, Crispy Middle Eastern Falafel
description: A post-workout French dish featuring fenugreek and other aromatic spices for a unique flavor profile that is both peppery and crispy.
instructions: Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and toast fenugreek, coriander, and cumin seeds for about 2 minutes or until fragrant.
In a large mixing bowl, combine ground chickpeas with the toasted seeds, salt, tahini sauce, garlic paste, and onion powder. Mix well to form a dough-like mixture.
Shape into small balls (about the size of an olive) using your hands. Flatten each ball slightly for better crispy texture.
Heat oil in another pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F (175°C). Fry falafel balls in batches, cooking for about 2-3 minutes on each side or until golden brown and crisp.
Remove from the pan using a slotted spoon and let them drain on paper towels. Sprinkle with sesame seeds before serving.
Serve hot as a snack alongside a piece of fresh fruit or vegetables.
